compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/bare soil. The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current occupancy.

plant_audit.p3_pothos: The specimen shows a single, chlorotic, pale leaf with a thin, struggling petiole. The plant is in a state of severe physiological decline, likely due to nutrient deficiency or light starvation.
plant_audit.p1_p2_p4: These pots contain only substrate and debris (eggshells). No viable plant tissue remains.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears consistently dark and potentially waterlogged or compacted. No signs of active growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P3/P4 area) suggests an attempt at calcium supplementation or soil amendment. A small electronic component (likely a header pin) has appeared in the CURRENT image.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though the lack of plant transpiration suggests high moisture retention in the soil.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-1: Minimal change; the Pothos leaf remains in a static, drooping posture.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The introduction of a small electronic component (header pin) on the desk surface near the pots is the only significant environmental change.

visual_health_inference.p3_pothos: Critical. The leaf is pale and lacks turgor pressure. The plant is failing to thrive in its current environment.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: The biome is effectively non-functional as a botanical display; it is currently a collection of empty pots with one failing specimen.

anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells as a soil additive.
Introduction of a small electronic header pin in the final frame.
Total absence of the registered P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a system in collapse. Of the four registered plants, only a single, sickly Pothos leaf remains in the P3/P4 cluster. The other pots are devoid of life, containing only soil and fragmented eggshells. The environment is static, with no signs of new growth or recovery. The recent addition of an electronic component suggests the desk is being used for non-botanical activities, further distancing the space from a healthy growing environment.
